prs.title: refactor: replace deprecated url.parse with WHATWG URL API
prs.body:
This PR addresses the open `DeprecationWarning` caused by Node's legacy URL API (as reported in #181) by replacing instances of `url.parse()` and `url.resolve()` with the modern, WHATWG-compliant `new URL()` API.

### Changes Made
- Removed the deprecated `require('url')` from [lib/strategy.js](cci:7://file:///Users/vikashkumar/Desktop/Passport/passport/passport-oauth2/lib/strategy.js:0:0-0:0).
- Replaced `url.parse(callbackURL)` block with `new URL()` validation logic.
- Replaced URL parameter injections with the modern `URLSearchParams` API. 
- Added a `.replace(/\+/g, '%20')` check against `parsed.href` output to accurately preserve the required space encoding (`%20`) and maintain 100% backward compatibility with the existing test suite.
### Testing
All 267 local tests run successfully without deprecation warnings.

prs.title: docs: modernize Mongoose examples using async/await
prs.body:
This PR updates the Mongoose examples in the [README.md](cci:7://file:///Users/vikashkumar/Desktop/Passport/passport/README.md:0:0-0:0). As of Mongoose 7.x+, `Model.findOne()` and `Model.findById()` no longer accept callbacks. The documentation examples have been updated to use modern `async/await` syntax to prevent confusion for new users setting up Passport alongside modern Mongoose.

### Changes Made
- Updated the `LocalStrategy` example in the **Strategies** section to use a `try...catch` block with `await User.findOne()`.
- Updated the `passport.deserializeUser` example in the **Sessions** section to use a `try...catch` block with `await User.findById()`.

prs.title: fix: throw Error on circular references in query filters and updates
prs.body:
Fixes #10378

Prior to this change, Mongoose would crash with a 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' error when circular data structures was passed as a query filter or update object.

This PR implements path tracking in recursive cloning/merging/casting helpers to detect and throw an Error instead of crashing.

Changes:
- lib/utils.js: Updated clonePOJOsAndArrays and merge to track seen ancestors.
- lib/helpers/clone.js: Updated clone/cloneObject/cloneArray to use a private _pathSeen argument for circularity detection.
- lib/cast.js: Added ancestors tracking in the main cast and _cast functions.

This ensures all standard entry points for user data are protected against stack overflow due to circularity.

prs.title: fix(regenerator): handle destructuring hoist crash in async (#17517)
prs.body:
## Problem
When running `@babel/plugin-transform-async-to-generator` followed by `@babel/plugin-transform-regenerator`, async code containing untransformed destructuring can crash with:

- `Property name expected type of string but got undefined`

Repro from `#17517`:

```js
const testA = async () => {
  return async () => {
    const { resData, config } = {}
    console.log('a', resData)
  }
}
```

## Cause
Regenerator's `hoist` pass assumed every `VariableDeclarator` has an `Identifier` id, and used `dec.id.name` when collecting vars. With destructuring, `dec.id` is a pattern so `name` is `undefined`, triggering the `@babel/types` invariant.

## Solution
Collect binding identifiers via `getBindingIdentifiers(dec.id)` instead of assuming `Identifier`, so patterns are supported and the transform no longer crashes.

## Tests
- Added a regression test covering the repro (`packages/babel-plugin-transform-regenerator/test/regression-17517.js`).

Fixes #17517

prs.title: fix(types): preserve empty import braces for TS verbatimModuleSyntax
prs.body:
## Problem
When TypeScript removes type-only import specifiers (e.g. `import { type T } from \"./x.js\"`), `verbatimModuleSyntax` preserves the *module import form* by emitting `import {} from \"./x.js\"` rather than normalizing it to a bare side-effect import `import \"./x.js\"`.

Babel currently cannot round-trip the distinction between `import {} from \"x\"` and `import \"x\"` because both are represented as `ImportDeclaration` nodes with an empty `specifiers` array. As a result, `@babel/plugin-transform-typescript` can end up printing `import \"./xyz.js\"` in cases where TypeScript (and tools like esbuild) would print `import {} from \"./xyz.js\"`.

This is visible in the repro from [#17843](https://github.com/babel/babel/issues/17843).

## Solution
- Track whether an import used empty braces (`import {} from \"x\"`) by recording `extra.emptyImportBraces` on `ImportDeclaration` nodes during parsing.
- Teach the generator to print `import {} from ...` when `extra.emptyImportBraces` is present.
- When `@babel/plugin-transform-typescript` removes the *last* import specifier (e.g. eliding `{ type T }`), mark the import so it prints as `import {} from ...` instead of being normalized to `import \"...\"`.

## Before
For the repro case (see `packages/babel-plugin-transform-typescript/test/fixtures/imports/verbatimModuleSyntax-empty-imports`), Babel could emit:

```js
import \"./xyz.js\";
```

## After
Babel preserves the empty-braces form:

```js
import {} from \"./xyz.js\";
```

## Tests
- Updated existing fixture that previously normalized `import {} from` to `import` side-effect form.
- Added a regression fixture mirroring the repro from #17843.
- Updated parser fixtures to assert the new `extra.emptyImportBraces` field.

## Additional small polish
- Adjusted the `MissingOneOfPlugins` diagnostic to say \"parser plugins\" instead of \"parser plugin(s)\" (fixtures updated accordingly).

Fixes #17843

prs.title: [tests] Fix strict flag assertion message
prs.body:
## Problem

In the `creation-data` test suite (`src/test/moment/creation-data.js`), the second assertion in the `strict` test verifies that `creationData().strict` is **false** when `moment` is called without the strict flag. However, the assertion message currently reads `strict is true`, which contradicts the actual expectation and can be misleading when the test fails.

## Solution

Update the assertion message for the non-strict case from `strict is true` to `strict is false` so that the human-readable message correctly reflects the expected value of the `strict` flag.

This change affects only the test description string and does not alter the test logic or runtime behavior.

## Impact

- **Clarity**: Makes the `strict` test easier to understand by aligning the assertion message with the actual condition being tested.
- **Debugging**: Reduces confusion when reading test failures, since the failure message will now accurately describe the expected behavior.

## Backward Compatibility

- No production code, public APIs, or typings are modified.
- The change is limited to a single test assertion message and is fully backward compatible.

prs.title: [misc] Clarify FAQ TypeScript section heading
prs.body:
## Problem

The TypeScript FAQ currently contains a section heading labeled `Case and Program`, which is confusing and does not accurately describe the content below it. The section simply links to several known GitHub issues, so the heading appears to be a typographical error and can be misleading to readers scanning the document.

## Solution

Rename the FAQ section heading from `Case and Program` to `Cases and problems` to better reflect that the section lists known issue cases and related problems.

This change is limited to a single Markdown heading in `FAQ.md`.

## Impact

- **Readability**: Makes the FAQ clearer for TypeScript users by using a more descriptive heading.
- **Maintainability**: Aligns documentation wording with the actual content, reducing potential confusion for future contributors and users.

## Notes

- This change only updates documentation and does **not** modify any runtime code or public APIs.
- Backward compatibility is fully preserved.

prs.title: fix(compiler): small improvement to error message handling
prs.body:
## What problem exists
A small typo/grammar issue in `parseErrorForMissingSemicolonAfter`'s documentation can be a minor distraction when working on parser diagnostics.

## Why this change is useful
This PR corrects wording in two nearby comments in `src/compiler/parser.ts`, making the intent clearer without changing any compiler behavior.

## Files modified
- `src/compiler/parser.ts`

## Tests
- Not added (comment-only change).
- Local verification: `npm run build`.
- Note: `npm test` fails locally with `EMFILE: too many open files, watch` in `unittests:: sys:: symlinkWatching::` (environment/FS watcher limit); the change does not affect runtime or test logic.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

prs.title: docs: update Node.js version requirement in README to match package.json
prs.body:
**Summary of the change**
This PR updates the README to accurately reflect the Node.js `>=8` requirement established in the `package.json` engines field, updating it from the previously stated Node 4 requirement. I also fixed a minor grammatical issue describing the operating system support.

**Why the change is needed**
To avoid confusion for new users trying to run the framework on older node versions and failing, the documentation should be kept in sync with the package dependency requirements.

**Implementation details**
- Updated the "Setup" section in the `README.md` file. 

**How it was tested**
The textlint suite was executed using `npm run textlint` to ensure the markdown is compliant. Additionally, manually confirmed that the `engines.node` within `package.json` correctly mandates Node 8 or higher.

**Any breaking changes**
No testing/runtime changes. Purely an update to human-readable markdown.

prs.title: refactor: remove lodash dependency in favor of native JavaScript
prs.body:
**Summary of the change**
This PR removes the `lodash` dependency from the `hoodie` package and replaces its specific usage with native equivalent operations (such as `Object.assign` and `Array.prototype.reduce`).

**Why the change is needed**
Modern Node.js versions have comprehensive support for common data manipulations that were historically handled by utility libraries. Removing `lodash`:
1. Decreases our total dependency count.
2. Narrows down potential security vulnerability surface area from sub-dependencies.
3. Slightly speeds up package installation time and reduces the overall size for end-users.

**Implementation details**
- **`cli/options.js`**: `pick()` requirement was dropped in favor of extracting the defaults with `Object.keys()` and filtering via `Array.prototype.reduce()`.
- **`server/index.js`**: The `_.cloneDeep()` and `_.defaultsDeep()` calls merged configurations (`plugins`, `app`, `paths`). Because we only override predefined 1-depth nested fields here, native `Object.assign` provides exactly the same deep-merging behavior we desired without tracking heavy `lodash` properties.
- **`test/unit/server/public-test.js`**: `_.merge` implementation changed to a native `Object.assign` alternative.

**How it was tested**
The native changes duplicate the exact execution states previously achieved using `lodash`. I ran the standard unit and integration test suites via `npm test` and formatting via `npm run pretest` without observing any functional regressions in CLI parameter parsing or Server initialization processes.

**Any breaking changes**
No. This change operates under identical input/output contracts to consumers and represents purely an under-the-hood modernization task.

prs.title: docs: add async/await timeout example in axios documentation
prs.body:
### Summary

This PR adds a missing example for handling request timeouts using async/await.  
The current Axios documentation demonstrates timeout usage, but does not include 
a clear async/await example with proper error handling through try/catch.

### What’s Added

- A new code snippet showing how to use `timeout` with async/await.
- Demonstrates how to detect timeout errors using `ECONNABORTED`.
- Keeps consistency with other examples in the documentation.

### Motivation

Issue #7225 requested a dedicated async/await timeout example because beginners 
often get confused about how timeouts work with promise-based error handling.  
This addition makes the docs clearer and more complete.

### Related Issue
Closes #7225
